eldora.note: 5:40 AM update – Good morning, skiers and riders! Welcome to the Eldora snow report for Friday, March 27, 2026. After a stretch of warm spring days, cooler temps are back with a high near 42 and skies clearing through the morning. Groomers are going to be in great shape today! And the weekend is shaping up to be a blast, with the Timbers Classic Rail Jam kicking off tomorrow at Woodward Timbers Park. Come watch some of the best park riders on the Front Range throw down! See ya out there! NWS Weather Snapshot: A 20 percent chance of snow showers before noon. Cloudy, then gradually becoming mostly sunny, with a high near 42. East southeast wind 5 to 8 mph, with gusts as high as 20 mph. Uphill and Nordic Skiing... The Nordic Center is now closed for the season. Check Nordic Pulse for the latest update. Uphill Access is now closed for the season. Visit eldora.com/uphill for more info. 2026-27 season passes are now on sale . Lock in your lowest price now, before prices increase on April 30. Stay in the loop: This page, eldora.com/conditions , is always your go-to for the latest updates, but you can also sign up for text alerts so you never miss a beat. Text one of the following keywords to (866) 965-6224: ELDORA for general alerts, UPHILL for uphill-specific updates, or NORDIC for Nordic updates. Message and data rates may apply. Thanks for checking the Eldora snow report. We'll see you on the hill! -- GENERAL INFORMATION Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week. Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week. Single-Occupancy Vehicle (SOV) Fees: Vehicles with two or more occupants may park for free seven days a week. Vehicles with one occupant will be charged $10 on weekends, during certain holiday periods, and on weekdays with 10 or more inches of new snow. Credit/debit is accepted; cash is not. High-Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) Parking: Per Boulder County requirements, the Elk Lot (nearest to Alpenglow chair) is reserved seven days a week for vehicles with three or more occupants on a first-come, first-served basis. Learn more here . EV Parking: Eldora provides 30 free ChargePoint EV charging stations. These premium parking spots are available on a first-come, first-served basis and are subject to the SOV policy above. To park in the EV lot, you MUST have an EV and be actively charging. If you are finished charging, please move your car to another non-EV space so others can use the chargers. Easy, Convenient RTD Bus Service from Boulder: RTD’s NB route brings you from Downtown Boulder Station straight to the Alpenglow chairlift in about 40 minutes; riders 19 and under ride RTD for FREE through RTD's Zero Fare for Youth program. For schedule and fare info, visit rtd-denver.com

killington.note: Good morning, Beast family, Yesterday's warmup brought more soft snow along with a great scene to enjoy your lunch on the deck. Today we’ll see clouds early before the sun fights its way out. Temps are in the mid 20s this morning and will be steadily dropping throughout the day, falling to around 20 at the base and 12 at the peak later in the day. A steady northwest wind ranging from 25-40 mph will keep things feeling a little brisk. With temperatures dropping overnight from around 45 degrees yesterday, surfaces conditions are shifting and are already beginning to firm up. To help mitigate any refreezing, our grooming team has employed a strategy to tackle higher elevations later in the night, as well as regrooming Great Eastern, much of Ramshead, Snowdon, Bear Mountain, and up to Killington Peak. They were on the usual plan, with trails like Bear Claw, Cruise Control, Bunny Buster, and many more getting resurfaced. Groomed trails will be the move today, through we're expecting variable conditions including machine groomed hard pack and frozen granular. We may see some trail closures due to frozen conditions, so head to our Lifts & Trails Report for the full list of options. Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our Canyon and South Ridge Quads coming online at 10:00 a.m. Please note, we may see some lift delays for regrooming this morning, so stay tuned. There’s a small cold snap coming in on Saturday, so you know what that means: snowmaking is coming back online on Superstar and Mouse Trap. It’s already spring, but we’re still keeping winter alive as long as we can! In kparks, the parks at Bear (Dream Maker & The Stash) will be closed this morning due to conditions with no time estimate to open. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. This weekend will be your last chance to take advantage of our Meet the Mountains Tours , taking place daily at 10:00 and 11:00 a.m., weather permitting. Meet your friendly tour guide in the courtyard at Snowshed Base Lodge and hit the slopes for a 75–90-minute tour of Killington Resort. Season passes for 26/27 are now on sale! Lock in the lowest price on Beast 365 or Winter Unlimited passes with lots of perks, plus free skiing & riding for the rest of this spring. Even better news for families: Youth & Child pass pricing has been significantly reduced for next season, with over $300 in savings on Beast 365 access. Have a fun Friday! - Evan Operating Day: 136 Lifts Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through March 28. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends. A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1! The Tubing Park presented by bubly is closed for the season. Uphill Travel Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific uphill route information, including a map of each route: Ramshead base to top of Ramshead Express Quad - Open Ramshead Express Quad to top of Snowdon Mountain - Open Snowshed base to the top of Snowshed Slope - Open Base of Sunrise Village Triple to top of Bear Mountain - Open Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect - CLOSED Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open Pico Summit Glade Route - CLOSED Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here . All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours. MOUNTAIN SERVICES Parking & Shuttles Parking is available at the K-1, Snowshed, Ramshead, Bear, Skyeship and Vale parking lots. Please respect signage and allow our parking staff to assist you on your trip to the slopes. Shuttle service is available from 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. midweek and 6:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends and holidays. Winter car camping and overnight parking is only available in the lot across from Skyeship Lodge. See details here . Lodge Services All lodges are open daily from 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekdays and 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends/peak dates*. K-1 Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. daily. Brew Bar (2nd floor): Open serving handcrafted coffees at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 7:30 a.m. on weekends, with cocktails and beers served starting at 9:00 a.m. K-1 Pub (3rd floor): Open at 10:00 a.m. daily. Rentals & Killington Sports at K-1: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Guest Services: 1st floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 1:00 p.m. on weekdays and until 4:30 p.m. on weekends/peak dates. 2nd floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Superstar Umbrella Bar: Open at 11:00 a.m. from Friday - Sunday offering beer, wine, liquor, and specialty hot cocktails. Snowshed Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. daily. Long Trail Pub (3rd floor): Open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., offering our Lunch Dining Menu until 5:30 p.m. and lite fare until 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Umbrella Bar: Open on Saturdays and Sundays serving beers, cocktails, and light fare. Rentals, Killington Sports at Snowshed, & Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Snowshed Demos & Tuning: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Ski & snowboard demo packages available. Please note that binding mounts and adjustments are not offered at this time. Click here for more information. Private & Adult Group Lessons: Booked online in advance here . Ramshead Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on Fridays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served daily with grab & go options & soups. The pizza station is open Thursday - Monday, and the full grill menu is available Friday - Sunday. Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 12:00 p.m. on Monday – Thursday and until 4:30 p.m. on Friday - Sunday at the ticket windows outside of the lodge. Rentals: Open for youth lesson participants and families only. Youth Lessons: Youth Group Lessons and our Ministars program meet at Ramshead. Child Care Center: Open weekdays from 8:30 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. and weekends from 8:00 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. Bear Mountain Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Open daily with grab & go items and soups available. Full grill menu available Friday - Sunday. Bear Bar: Open from 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m. on weekdays and until 5:00 p.m. on weekends. Guest Services: Available Friday - Monday at ticket windows outside the lodge. Open 30 minutes before lifts open. Kiosk lift ticket pickup is available daily. Skyeship Lodge Food & Bar Service: Available daily from The Rollin’ Rooster. Guest Services: Open daily inside the lodge. Closing Date: March 29th On-Mountain Dining Options Peak Lodge – Open daily from 9:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. Grill open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. Peak Lodge Bar offers its own menu daily with QR code ordering. Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill – Ski-on, ski- off. Open Thursday-Monday from 11:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. weather permitting. Ledgewood Yurt – closed for the season. Mountain services are subject to change. Please check here daily before making your lunch and lodge choices. Winter Lift Tickets and rentals must be purchased online at this link . Check to see if your pass is good to go using our Online Pass Checker . Find Season Pass FAQs by clicking here . Get your Unlimited Winter Season Pass today! Buy now . Have a ticketing question? Our text number ( 802-523-2202 ) is the best way to get it answered quickly, but please note that we cannot process transactions via text. Have a season pass question? Call 1-800-621-MTNS or email seasonpass@killington.com Please visit Killington.com/cancellation for more details on our cancellation policy. Killington Sports at Route 4 is open Sunday - Thursday from 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m., Friday and Saturday from 10:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., and 10:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. on holidays. Killington is a cashless resort. We accept all major credit cards as well as Apple, Google, and Samsung Pay. *No remaining Peak Dates during winter 2025/26. We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

copper mountain.note: Another beautiful day to ski and ride! Clear skies with high temps in the upper-40s. Winds will be from the southwest around 5-10 mph, gusting into the 20s. Be careful out there and get ready to watch USASA National Championships starting tomorrow. Check out all the Copper Mountain events happening today and this week: https://www.coppercolorado.com/events-activities/events-calendar/ Happy Friday!

snowbird.note: Happy Friday! It’s Cassidy with your snow report. Mountain Update: 8 am - 2 pm spring lift operations will remain in place through Sunday, March 29. Hours of operation will be reassessed for the following week. Good morning, good morning, good morning! We’ve got another beautiful day on our hands. Maybe hit a few mineral laps in the morning to find the soft snow in the sun and then make your way towards Peruvian and Gad Valley as it warms up. Bring your sunscreen people; goggle tans are cool, but goggle burns don’t feel quite as nice. How’s that song go? Oh yeah, It’s Friday, Friday, gotta get down on Friday. Rebecca Black said it, not me. What better way to spend it than getting on up to The Bird? Today kicks off the first day of Plazapalooza . Wasatch Valley Drifters will be jamming out on the Plaza Deck. Please note, due to current operating hours, Plazapalooza will run from 1-4pm this weekend only. You can also catch the Steve Young Ski Classic happening on Chickadee today and tomorrow with Wasatch Adaptive Sports. If you haven't yet gotten your Chickadee Fever badge on the Snowbird App, now's the time. The Chickadee Lift will close for the 2025-26 season for replacement on April 6th. The new Chickadee Lift is expected to reopen for summer operations in July. ALL uphill travel is closed. Don’t be that guy (or gal). Please remember to ski and ride in control, if you don’t, you’re gonna have a bad time. See you out there!
